##### About me

Amanda G
Doula Services
Birth and Postpartum Services
Placenta Encapsulation
Prenatal and Parent&Me Yoga Instructor

I am so honored to do this work. I am here to guide you through the journey of pregnancy, birth, and motherhood. Birth is a sacred ceremony. Whether you choose to birth in a hospital, birth center or at home, each is beautiful and deserving of celebration. As your doula, I believe in your vision, I trust in your strength, and I remind you of the power within you that has been passed down from generation to generation.

##### Education

Bachelor's of Science (2011)

##### Service introduction

Hi! My name is Amanda McNair-Robinson and I've been a doula since 2017. I've attended over 100 births. I have also assisted in hospitals, birth centers and at home. I have helped many clients have successful VBACs.

KAISER and IEHP offer FREE services for its members. The services include prenatal visits, birth support and postpartum visits. Please reach out if you'd like to know more! My email is amandagdoula@gmail.com.

[Skip to Content](https://city.milwaukee.gov/Health/Services-and-Programs/MCH/BOMB#contentArea) ![](https://city.milwaukee.gov/ImageLibrary/MKE-Health1/HEH-Website-2024/images/MHD_Health_icon_background_navy-01.png) # Birth Outcomes Made Better (BOMB) Doula Program ## What is the BOMB Doula Program? ![Woman holds baby in her arms](https://city.milwaukee.gov/ImageLibrary/MKE-Health1/FCH-website-updates-2025/BOMB-Doula/Employee-Headshots/img_0450_720.jpg?MediumSquare) The Birth Outcomes Made Better (BOMB) Doula Program was created to support healthy babies in the City of Milwaukee. Our teams empower families with the guidance and education necessary for a healthy pregnancy and beyond. The program is available to all City of Milwaukee residents who are at or before 30 weeks pregnant, no insurance required. Doulas work with families during the prenatal period, through birth, and for 12 weeks postpartum. ## Client Intake Information ![Two women smile while holding a baby](https://city.milwaukee.gov/ImageLibrary/MKE-Health1/FCH-website-updates-2025/BOMB-Doula/Employee-Headshots/img_1306_720.jpg?MediumSquare) The BOMB Doula program is now accepting referrals from August and beyond. [Fill out our form](https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=WWSIOHdfIka5DkNsmUulAVlwjFm0iAVOiQdhlC7hRN9UQzAwRURGRUhYVEhUMlVUT05JRklZR0xOVy4u) ## [Join our FREE Monthly Breastfeeding Webinar](https://us02web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZIscOyhqzotEtFR1S5qGfPn-UtWhZUWYz7_?_x_zm_rtaid=RQeE6OFJTmabPf2aKEVk5w.1723561861504.c0739cadd1c40b365eb614a61a6c6247&_x_zm_rhtaid=380\#/registration) If you're a new parent, expecting, or just seeking more knowledge, this webinar will help guide you through the breastfeeding journey! - Every 4th Thursday of the month, 5:00 - 6:30 p.m. - Topics alternate each month: ## Breastfeeding 101 ![](https://city.milwaukee.gov/ImageLibrary/MKE-Health1/MCH/BOMB-Doula/mom-breastfeeding-child-1.jpg?MediumSquare) Get expert tips to start breastfeeding confidently **[VIEW SCHEDULE](https://us02web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZIscOyhqzotEtFR1S5qGfPn-UtWhZUWYz7_?_x_zm_rtaid=RQeE6OFJTmabPf2aKEVk5w.1723561861504.c0739cadd1c40b365eb614a61a6c6247&_x_zm_rhtaid=380#/registration)** ## Pumping 101 ![](https://city.milwaukee.gov/ImageLibrary/MKE-Health1/MCH/BOMB-Doula/breast-milk-pumping-2.jpg?MediumSquare) Learn about pumping and storing milk effectively **[VIEW SCHEDULE](https://us02web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZ0vf-CrqzkrGNMYsqXikLeQAfYEI2WdOpR4#/registration)** ## Our Partnerships Scroll table to view all | | | | --- | --- | | ![](https://city.milwaukee.gov/ImageLibrary/MKE-Health1/FCH-website-updates-2025/BOMB-Doula/Employee-Headshots/partner-logos-02.png?Small) | ![](https://city.milwaukee.gov/ImageLibrary/MKE-Health1/FCH-website-updates-2025/BOMB-Doula/Employee-Headshots/partner-logos-01.png?Small) | | [Milwaukee County\

Mar 11, 2022









Amber provided doula services for me during my recent birth. Prior to the birth, she visited my husband and I and trained us on pain relief techniques that turned out to be absolutely vital in having an unmedicated hospital delivery. She also taught us some useful diapering and swaddling techniques. In the weeks leading up to my due date, she coached me via text through prodromal labor and helped me understand what was happening. On the day of the birth, she was my anchor and helped me stay calm through my pain and have a successful natural birth. She always knew just what to say, how to touch me, and what would help. I strongly recommend Amber for your birthing journey.

#### About Momo

Hello everyone, my name is Momo Bass and I am a certified birth doula as well as a trained postpartum doula that serves the greater Los Angeles and Pasadena area. Aside from doing this rewarding work for awhile, I am also a mama myself, to three amazing kiddos! When I'm not cheering on my kids at soccer practice, you'll likely find me tending to my ever-growing houseplant collection, or tending to myself at the gym. But my true passion lies in supporting families embarking on their incredible birthing journey. Motherhood is what threw me into the thralls of the what it means to bring life into this world. It's this firsthand knowledge, coupled with my relentless curiosity about the miracle of birth and the transition into becoming a parent, that led me to become a birth and postpartum doula. I am coming out of a hiatus since having my son, but have been very active within this past year.

##### **Care philosophy**

Bringing my kiddos earth side became my superpower, and it’s become my mission to ignite that same fire in the families I work with. I believe that starts with finding judgment free informational and emotional support, and I believe it starts when parents take back control of how their labor & postpartum experience goes by making informed decisions on what’s best for yourself and your family.

#### About Kate

At the heart of my doula practice is the belief that birth is not only a profound physical experience but also a radical, political, and deeply hopeful act. Every birth is unique, and every birthing person deserves care that honors their individuality, values, and choices. I am here to provide continuous, compassionate support—wherever and however you choose to give birth.

My approach is rooted in flexibility. Whether you're planning a hospital birth, a home birth, or something in between, I walk with you every step of the way. I’m comfortable in a variety of settings and work seamlessly alongside medical providers, midwives, nurses, and OBs. I believe in collaborative care and see myself as part of a team that centers you—your comfort, your autonomy, your voice.

As your doula, I support your decisions and help you understand your options throughout your pregnancy, labor, and early postpartum period. From evidence-based information about induction and pain relief to navigating unexpected changes in your birth plan, I provide judgment-free guidance. I won’t make decisions for you, but I will ensure you feel informed, heard, and empowered in every moment.

Advocacy is a cornerstone of my practice. That means I help amplify your voice in spaces where it might otherwise be quieted. It means creating space for you to ask questions, push back, change your mind, or stand firm. It means respecting your intuition and experience, even (and especially) when it doesn’t fit the textbook.

Continuity of care matters. Our relationship doesn’t begin and end in the delivery room. From our first prenatal meeting through to postpartum check-ins, I’m a consistent, calming presence. I get to know your story, your fears, your hopes—and I carry them with me as I support you through this life-changing transition.

I am committed to being fully present. I listen deeply. I hold space without judgment. I honor the sacred and the messy. I believe birth is a site of transformation—a moment where the world cracks open and something entirely new begins. That deserves reverence, support, and care that reflects its intensity and power.

Birth is political. It intersects with race, class, gender, ability, and access. I strive to hold an inclusive, anti-oppressive practice. All are welcome here: all genders, all bodies, all families, all birth plans. I continuously work to unlearn bias and practice in a way that supports reproductive justice and birth equity.

Birth is hopeful. It asks us to believe in something unseen, to trust the process of change. As a doula, I hold that hope with you. I believe in your strength, your capacity, and your right to a birth experience that feels safe, supported, and yours.

No matter what your path looks like, I am here to walk it with you.

##### **Care philosophy**

Every birth is a story. I’m here to help you tell yours. My journey toward becoming a doula began with my first pregnancy 15 years ago. I became obsessed with the power and transformation of pregnancy and birth. Four babies of my own later and being honored to be chosen to sit with, support, and witness the births of friends and family along the way, I decided I just wasn’t quite done.

## Doula Services: FAQs

What is a Doula and What Can I Expect from a Birth Doula?

A doula is a trained professional who provides personalized emotional, physical, and informational support during pregnancy, childbirth, and the postpartum period. Unlike medical professionals, doulas do not perform clinical tasks. Instead, they focus on empowering and supporting individuals through their unique birthing journey.

When you hire a birth doula, you can expect continuous, compassionate care tailored to your needs. From pregnancy through labor and delivery, they work closely with you to understand your preferences, alleviate anxieties, and help you make informed decisions. Doulas provide evidence-based information about birthing techniques, pain management options, and medical interventions to guide you in creating a birth experience aligned with your goals.

During labor, a birth doula offers hands-on physical support, such as massage, counterpressure, and guidance on effective birthing positions. They also help you manage discomfort through relaxation and breathing techniques, ensuring a calm and empowering environment. Importantly, they serve as your advocate, helping communicate your birth plan to the medical team so your preferences are understood and respected.

Doulas also support your partner by involving them in the process, offering practical tips for comfort measures, and stepping in so they can rest when needed. After the birth, your doula remains by your side during the postpartum period, assisting with bonding, breastfeeding, or simply ensuring you feel comfortable and cared for.

In essence, a doula’s unwavering support transforms childbirth into a more positive, confident, and empowering experience, allowing you to focus on welcoming your baby with joy.

How do I hire a doula in Fontana, CA?

There are two ways you can hire a doula through Meela in Fontana, CA.

You can browse through the available doulas in Fontana, CA and send the doulas you are interested in a message, introducing yourself and setting up an interview. We recommend interviewing at least three before hiring your top doula.

You can also hire a doula in Fontana, CA in three easy steps:

1. Post a job for free: answer some questions and receive responses from top-rated doulas in Fontana, CA. It takes less than 30 seconds to [post your job](https://www.hellomeela.com/post-a-job/).

2. Get your list of Fontana, CA doulas: in a few hours, you will have a collection of doulas near you who are qualified to meet your needs. You can compare pricing, experience and read reviews. You can chat with each doula to learn more about them.

3. Hire a doula with confidence: schedule virtual or in-person interviews with your top doula choices. Hire your top doula and start getting the care you deserve.

How much does a doula cost in Fontana, CA?

While the cost of a doula can vary based on experience and the services offered, you can expect the average cost of a doula in Fontana, CA to be $1,214, which is the average cost of a doula in the state of California. The cost of a doula in Fontana, CA can range from $997 to $1,431. These costs are based on a representative group of 1,036 doulas surveyed by Meela across the state of California. The average cost of a doula in California is higher than the U.S. national average cost of a doula, which is $971.

When hiring a birth doula in Fontana, CA, you and your doula will discuss and agree upon a fee structure, and your doula will send you itemized invoices through Meela, which you can pay securely through the app.

Will insurance cover the cost of a doula in Fontana, CA?

Some insurance companies will cover all or part of the cost of hiring a doula. Contact your insurance company to find out if they offer any coverage for hiring a doula; not all insurance companies do.

What types of doulas are for hire in Fontana, CA?

You can hire two types of doulas in Fontana, CA: a birth doula and a postpartum doula. The birth doula is during your pregnancy and through the birth of your child. They provide you with emotional, physical and educational support during your pregnancy, your labor and delivery and for the first few hours after your child is born.

A postpartum doula will support you for the first few weeks after the baby is born. They provide in-home assistance, including teaching you how to take care of your newborn, acting as lactation consultant, and can provide some night nanny services.

Can a doula help with a C-section birth?

Yes, a birth doula can provide support during a Cesarean birth and delivery. Doulas are non-medical professionals and do not replace obstetricians, physicians, midwives or nurses. They are an additive member to the team and provide emotional, physical and informational support to the birthing parent, partner and family. It is up to you whether the certified doula can remain in the operating room during the procedure.

What should I expect from a doula?

When you hire a doula in Fontana, CA, your services typically include several in-person prenatal sessions, 24/7 phone and text support, full attendance throughout your labor, and several hours of support immediately following your birth.

A doula in Fontana, CA can help you in any kind of birth. Certified doulas are able to assist parents in creating the ideal “birth environment” and help parents deliver their baby safely. A doula can provide support during the birth whether it occurs in a hospital, a home, or another birth center of some kind. Doulas are there to support you in your desired birth preferences, whether that is a preference for natural, non-medicated, assisted, C-section or anything else.

Certified doulas can also act as a liaison between you and hospital staff regarding your childbirth expectations and preferences. For example, if your preference is to have a non-medicated birth, a doula can help guide you through that process and create a personalized birth plan. A certified doula in Fontana, CA does not replace medical staff (such as your obstetrician and nurses).

When a surgical procedure is required, there are some situations in which a birth doula can also remain in the operating room, although this is not an expected obligation. A certified birth doula can also assist hospital staff to improve the overall birthing experience. A doula in Fontana, CA will typically stay with the parents for several hours after birth to help them with birth recovery.

Can a doula improve my birthing experience?

Evidence suggests that a birth doula in Fontana, CA can help improve parent satisfaction with their baby’s birth and delivery. The involvement of a doula can also lead to shorter deliveries with [less complications](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3647727/).

Recent studies show that parents who have a support person like a doula by their side throughout labor and childbirth are more likely to:

1. Give birth without the need for pain medicine
2. Describe their birth experience in a positive way
3. Give birth without the need for medical intervention, such as C-sections or vacuum delivery
4. Experience a shorter labor

When should I hire a doula?

You can hire a doula early in your pregnancy to allow you time to get to know each other and to begin planning for your later trimesters, labor, birth, and aftercare. It is ideal to hire a doula when you are around 20 weeks along, but you can start looking for one before that.

It is never too late to hire a doula in Fontana, CA; you have right up to your due date to find a doula near you and receive all the benefits of doula support.

What is the difference between a midwife and a doula?

While many people think that a doula and a midwife are the same thing, their educational background and job duties are different. Doulas are non-medical professionals who provide emotional, physical and educational support, while also offering actionable advice and guidance to new parents about how to nurture their baby.

A midwife is a certified nurse-midwife (CNM) or a certified midwife (CM). CNMs are medical professionals that are certified and licensed by the American College of Nurse-Midwives. CMs are also certified, but they do not have nursing backgrounds.

Doulas are not medical professionals; they focus on the emotional support for the parents and the family before, during, and after the birth of the baby. Their job is to provide information, advice, and support to the family not diagnose any medical conditions or provide medical advice.

## Find a DONA Certified Doula

## Benefits of Working with a DONA Certified Doula

DONA-certified doulas are trained to provide evidence-based, ethical, client-centered support. Certification must be earned and maintained through continued education and evaluation, ensuring professionalism and compassionate care throughout the birth or postpartum experience.

## How to Choose a DONA Certified Doula

Choosing the right doula is personal and can influence your birth or postpartum journey. Clarifying your needs and values can help guide your search.

## Decide what type of doula you need.

Birth doulas are trained to provide physical, emotional, and informational support throughout labor and delivery. The doula helps with comfort measures like positioning, counter pressure, and breathing techniques, offers emotional support to create a nurturing space for the birthing parent and their partner, and serves as a bridge of communication to help families make informed decisions and advocate for the very best care.

Postpartum doulas are trained to understand what new babies – and new parents – truly need. The doula helps with soothing techniques, offers lactation or bottle feeding support, and explains normal newborn behavior and postpartum recovery expectations.

Examples:

- Birth (CD/AdvCD)
- Postpartum (PCD/AdvPCD)

### Define Your Priorities

- Do I want someone with specific experience (home birth, hospital, first-time parent)?
- Is cultural, spiritual or language alignment important to me?
- What type of support do I expect most: emotional, informational, advocacy or hands-on?

## Search for Doulas

Use DONA International’s directory to find certified doulas by location and specialty, then interview candidates to ensure the right fit.

## Trusted Training & Professional Standards

DONA International doulas complete extensive training and follow professional guidelines designed to support families safely and respectfully.

- Rigorous certification process requiring hands-on experience and recertification every 3 years
- Certification includes a Code of Ethics & Standards of Practice for client-centered care
- Continuing education encourages up-to-date, research-based support
- Peer support network that fosters mentorship, collaboration and referrals

[Discover Our Resources](https://dona.org/find-a-dona-certified-doula/#)

# Doula services

At Kaiser Permanente, you’re welcome to have a doula on your support team during and after pregnancy and birth. A doula can help you stay informed, prepare for birth, manage labor pain, and recover after pregnancy.

![Doula helping a person in labor](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/content/dam/kporg/final/images/people/lifestyle/three-or-more/doula-helping-a-person-in-labor-l-dt.jpg)

## What is a doula?

A doula is a trained, nonmedical professional. They provide companionship and give physical, emotional, and informational support during pregnancy, birth, and postpartum recovery. Doulas focus on your well-being and offer consistent support through all stages of pregnancy and birth, including in cases of pregnancy loss or abortion.

## How a doula can support you

A doula can support you and your family during pregnancy, birth, and the postpartum period. They can help you understand your care options and review your [birth preferences](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/health-wellness/healtharticle.creating-your-birth-preferences-plan), which you’ll share with your care team. Doulas won’t make clinical decisions for you, but they can help you communicate with your care team.

Doulas support you in creating a calm environment to help labor progress. People who give birth with doulas tend to have shorter labors and fewer cesarean deliveries, and they’re less likely to ask for pain-relief medication.1

Doulas can offer various types of support, including:

- **Preparation —** Helping you understand and prepare for the birth process
- **Birth preferences —** Helping you decide on your preferences for your birth experience, including pain management and newborn feeding
- **Labor coaching —** Offering nonmedical techniques (such as touch, positioning, and breathing exercises) to help you relax, cope with pain, and progress through labor
- **Postpartum support —** Providing guidance with breastfeeding and newborn care, as well as support transitioning to life with your new baby

* * *

Read more

## **How to find a Kaiser Permanente–contracted doula**

Selecting a doula is an important and personal decision. Here are some steps to help you find a doula to support your birth experience with Kaiser Permanente.

1. **Search for doulas near you.** Review the [Kaiser Permanente Doula Services directory﻿](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/content/dam/kporg/final/documents/directories/regional-directories/doula-services-scal-en.pdf "Opens in a new window, external") for contracted doulas in your area. Doulas are listed by county. Details include their phone number and what health plans they accept.
2. **Interview doulas.** Set up phone calls with a few doulas. Ask about their experience, philosophy, and approach to birth.
3. **Make your decision.** Choose the Kaiser Permanente–contracted doula who best fits your needs. You can switch to a different contracted doula if your preferences change.

## What to look for in a doula

It’s important to choose a doula you feel comfortable with. Select a doula contracted by Kaiser Permanente, and make sure they work near your home and the hospital where you plan to deliver. They should also be available on your estimated due date.

Doulas we’ve contracted with will have training or experience in:

- Prenatal support
- The process of pregnancy and birth
- Nonmedical comfort measures and labor support techniques
- Breastfeeding support
- Adult/infant cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) certification
- Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) privacy rules

## Find out if your plan covers doula services

Services from doulas contracted by Kaiser Permanente are covered at no extra cost for California members on specific plans.2

We’re here to help you get the care you need. If you’d like to talk with someone about doula services, benefits, or claims, call Member Services at [**1-800-464-4000**](tel:+1-800-464-4000) (TTY [**711**](tel:711)), 24 hours a day, 7 days a week (except major holidays). Or see your _Evidence of Coverage_ for details about your plan’s coverage.

## Common questions

![expand](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/etc.clientlibs/settings/wcm/designs/kporg/kp-foundation/clientlib-modules/styleguide/resources/assets/icons/pluscircle.svg)![collapse](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/etc.clientlibs/settings/wcm/designs/kporg/kp-foundation/clientlib-modules/styleguide/resources/assets/icons/minuscirclesolid.svg)
Can I work with a doula who isn’t contracted by Kaiser Permanente or listed in the directory?


Services from doulas not listed in the [Kaiser Permanente Doula Services directory﻿](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/content/dam/kporg/final/documents/directories/regional-directories/doula-services-scal-en.pdf "Opens in a new window, external") won’t be covered. You can choose a different doula, but you’ll need to pay for their services yourself.3

![expand](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/etc.clientlibs/settings/wcm/designs/kporg/kp-foundation/clientlib-modules/styleguide/resources/assets/icons/pluscircle.svg)![collapse](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/etc.clientlibs/settings/wcm/designs/kporg/kp-foundation/clientlib-modules/styleguide/resources/assets/icons/minuscirclesolid.svg)
What doula services aren’t covered?


Some services, including but not limited to those listed below, aren’t covered under your doula benefit. If you get these services from a doula, you’ll need to pay for them.

- Any services from a doula who isn’t contracted by Kaiser Permanente
- Clinical or medical services (like taking blood pressure or temperature, fetal heart tone checks, vaginal exams, or postpartum clinical care)
- Help with activities of daily living (like shopping or laundry)
- Alternative or complementary services (like aromatherapy, birth education, or massage therapy)
- Yoga
- Birthing ceremonies (like sealing or closing the bones)
- Over-the-counter supplies or drugs
- Home birth
- Belly binding (traditional or ceremonial)
- Massage (maternal or infant)
- Photography
- Placenta encapsulation
- Vaginal steams
- Group classes on baby wearing

![expand](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/etc.clientlibs/settings/wcm/designs/kporg/kp-foundation/clientlib-modules/styleguide/resources/assets/icons/pluscircle.svg)![collapse](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/etc.clientlibs/settings/wcm/designs/kporg/kp-foundation/clientlib-modules/styleguide/resources/assets/icons/minuscirclesolid.svg)
What’s my cost for Kaiser Permanente–contracted doula services?


If your plan covers doula services, there’s no copay, deductible, or coinsurance. Kaiser Permanente won’t bill you for covered services from a contracted doula.

![expand](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/etc.clientlibs/settings/wcm/designs/kporg/kp-foundation/clientlib-modules/styleguide/resources/assets/icons/pluscircle.svg)![collapse](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/etc.clientlibs/settings/wcm/designs/kporg/kp-foundation/clientlib-modules/styleguide/resources/assets/icons/minuscirclesolid.svg)
Where can I receive services from my Kaiser Permanente–contracted doula?


You can get prenatal and postpartum doula services virtually or in person. In-person visits can be anywhere, including in your home or at the doula’s office. Doulas can also come with you to appointments with your Kaiser Permanente care team. During your labor and birth, your doula should support you in person.

![expand](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/etc.clientlibs/settings/wcm/designs/kporg/kp-foundation/clientlib-modules/styleguide/resources/assets/icons/pluscircle.svg)![collapse](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/etc.clientlibs/settings/wcm/designs/kporg/kp-foundation/clientlib-modules/styleguide/resources/assets/icons/minuscirclesolid.svg)
Do I need a referral for doula services?


No. You don’t need to ask your doctor to see a doula.

If you plan to have a doula support you during labor and birth, let your care team know ahead of time and mention your doula in your [birth preferences](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/health-wellness/healtharticle.putting-together-your-birth-preferences).

![expand](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/etc.clientlibs/settings/wcm/designs/kporg/kp-foundation/clientlib-modules/styleguide/resources/assets/icons/pluscircle.svg)![collapse](https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/etc.clientlibs/settings/wcm/designs/kporg/kp-foundation/clientlib-modules/styleguide/resources/assets/icons/minuscirclesolid.svg)
What’s the difference between a doula and a midwife?


Doulas aren’t medical providers. Midwives and doctors are licensed medical professionals who provide prenatal, childbirth, and postpartum clinical care.
